backend/ssot/biomarkers.yaml line 713: tsh registered as canonical biomarker. Signal libraries reference metric_id: tsh in override_rules conditions. TSH lab-range-based gating will use tsh as the metric_id.

backend/core/analytics/signal_evaluator.py lines 398–484 (evaluate_all), lines 419–440 (lab_range_exceeded path). CONFIRMED: evaluate_all() evaluates lab_range_exceeded on primary metric FIRST (line 422), producing a signal_state if primary marker is out of range. Override rules run AFTER (line 435) and only escalate state — they CANNOT block emission. Therefore or_free_t3_high_with_tsh_low (FT3 high signal_library) and or_free_t4_high_with_tsh_low (FT4 high signal_library) are escalation-only. If TSH is absent or non-suppressed, the signal STILL emits at 'suboptimal'. This is the exact blocker confirmed in batch2_remainder_resolution_register_v1.yaml lines 28–33. MANDATORY: TSH gating must be implemented as a PRE-EMISSION gate in evaluate_all(), checked after signal_state is determined but BEFORE appending to results. The gate must be fail-closed: if TSH is absent (_resolve_condition_metric_value returns None at line 281–282), gate fails and signal is not emitted.

changes.citation: knowledge_bus/packages/pkg_kb47_free_t4_low_thyroid_hormone_deficiency/signal_library.yaml line 50: activation_config.enable_lower_bound: false. signal_evaluator.py lines 179–185: lower-bound activation only fires when enable_lower_bound is true. With enable_lower_bound: false, FT4 low will NEVER emit via lab_range_exceeded even after runtime activation — permanently silenced. trigger_direction: low (signal_library.yaml line 14) confirms this package is intended to fire below lab range minimum. Cursor MUST set enable_lower_bound: true for pkg_kb47_free_t4_low_thyroid_hormone_deficiency. This is IN SCOPE: 'package signal metadata/rule metadata for the three eligible thyroid packages' (prompt line 309). Note: pkg_kb47_free_t3_low_low_t3_syndrome has the same issue (line 50) but is not being activated in this sprint — must remain untouched.

Clinical sign-off lines 7–10 + prompt lines 204–253. FT3 high: TSH must be LOW/SUPPRESSED (below lab range minimum — lab_range_boundary: lower). Block if TSH absent OR TSH not below min. FT4 high: same gate as FT3 high. FT4 low: TSH must be PRESENT (any value — block only if absent). Do NOT require TSH to be high for FT4 low gate; low FT4 with non-high TSH may indicate central hypothyroidism (prompt lines 247–248). Existing or_free_t4_low_with_tsh_high override rule correctly escalates state when TSH IS high — this is separate from the mandatory presence gate. For FT3 low: NO gate change, NO activation, NO modification.
